crime: Her friend asked months ago for the dinner table cleared each night; she said yes, and every evening since, when the hall empties, the pots and trays carry themselves back across it — a spoken yes keeps running. The other girls passed her their chores in turn; she never refused.
Her friend's requests had dwindled to nothing over weeks. That evening, the friend handed a torn hem to another girl for mending, in front of her, and never turned to her.
Each evening, the friend ate alone after everyone else had left the hall.
That night the clearing ran as always, but the soup pot broke from its nightly line, rose above the friend's seat, and overturned on her — boiling. She was in the lounge among the other girls.
At the morning meal, the girls found the friend at her seat beneath the overturned pot, scalded across the back from above. Her palms had no marks.
A girl who pulls a pot down on herself normally takes the scald on her hands and chest; the friend never touched it. The spill fell two tables off the line the clearing takes every night.
Her clearing runs as she intends; no one else directs it. The pot left its fixed nightly line because she steered it — thus the scald was deliberate.
fields.epitaph: Every favor kept, and no one kept her.
execution: An iron armature closed around her limbs and back, jointed at the arms, spine, and knees.
Whatever motion she begins, the frame carries it to its end and locks it there.
She shifted an arm; the frame drove it straight and fixed it. She eased her knees; the frame folded them down and held them.
She sagged; the frame folded her chest shut and locked. Breath stopped.

magic.name: Standing Yes
magic.text: A favor she agrees to aloud carries itself out, without her hands or her presence.
